name: Sweet Pepperbush
summary: Fragrant white flower spikes in July–August fill the high-summer nectar gap when little else is blooming. Also called summersweet — a name that names itself the moment you walk past one in bloom. Acid wet woods and stream edges.
notes: Heavy bumblebee, butterfly, and hummingbird use during the late-summer dearth. pH 4.5–6.0 — ideal for Pocono Plateau acid soils. Suckers to form colonies; excellent for stabilizing wet edges. Yellow fall color. Range-edge in Wayne County but well within hardiness.

refs.label: PSU Extension — Native Shrubs for Pollinators

planting.aspect: Stream edges; wet hollows; partly shaded acid wet woods
planting.site_notes: Plant in colonies of 5+ for visual mass and pollinator value. Wire cage helpful; moderate deer browse. Pairs with winterberry for late-summer + winter wildlife value in the same wet site.
